human_verification.notes: Reviewed food-labeling-specific boundaries, FDA and FSIS jurisdiction handling, allergen and ingredient evidence review, label revision control, lot-traceability dependencies, scenario-to-ability coverage, and refusal of legal, supplier-approval, and final release authority.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and manufacturing-and-supply-chain research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus manufacturing-and-supply-chain are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to food-labeling-specific regulatory process and evidence support, including ingredient, allergen, nutrition, claims-adjacent review, artwork revision, traceability linkage, and quality-hold boundary handling.
Authority sources cover FDA food labeling, allergen guidance, preventive controls, food traceability, GS1 lot-traceability vocabulary, and FSIS jurisdiction boundaries with refresh and decay rules.
Scenario set covers missing evidence, conflicting source or policy handling, jurisdiction conflict, legal or formal signoff requests, supplier substitution, traceability breaks, and tenant fact gaps.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; jurisdiction and tenant-specific differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
